invariant_definition: One of three intrinsic classes of *jīva* in Madhva's *taratamya*-doctrine. *Tamo-yogya* souls are constitutively fated to descend to *andha-tamas* (eternal darkness — Madhva's analogue of permanent hell). The classification is intrinsic, not earned. This is the most controversial of Madhva's doctrines and the principal Indian philosophical analogue to Calvinist double predestination — though arrived at on quite different metaphysical premises (*taratamya* of *svarūpa*, not divine election).

per_school.definition: *Tamo-yogya* souls' fate is *andha-tamas* — eternal darkness, the Mādhva equivalent of permanent damnation. The doctrine is grounded in metaphysical *taratamya*: just as some souls are intrinsically *mukti-yogya*, some are intrinsically *tamo-yogya*. The doctrine has been a perennial polemical target across Indian philosophy.

translator_note: 'Fit-for-darkness' is the literal gloss. The doctrine of constitutive eternal damnation should not be softened — it is precisely what Madhva asserts and what most Vedāntic schools reject.
